data.title: In HelloNation, Fireplace Expert Jimmy Smith of North Charleston Explains Fireplace Construction Planning
data.description:
In HelloNation, Fireplace Expert Jimmy Smith of North Charleston Explains Fireplace Construction Planning

In HelloNation, Fireplace Expert Jimmy Smith of North Charleston Explains Fireplace Construction Planning

PR Newswire

NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C., April 8, 2026

NORTH CHARLESTON, S.C., April 8, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- How can homeowners and builders avoid costly mistakes when adding a fireplace to a new home? A HelloNation article featuring Fireplace Expert Jimmy Smith of WilliamSmith Fireplaces of SC answers this question by emphasizing that fireplace construction planning must begin early in the building process. The feature explains how new home fireplace design requires careful attention to structure, venting, building codes, and finishing details to ensure safety and efficiency.

The article highlights that a fireplace is not just an aesthetic feature but also a mechanical system. Each decision, from wood-burning fireplace installation to electric fireplace installation, affects multiple trades. Framing, HVAC, electrical, masonry, and plumbing teams all rely on clear plans. By addressing the type and placement of the fireplace during the initial design phase, builders avoid conflicts later and preserve both architectural flow and mechanical performance.

Selecting the type of fireplace sets the course for construction. Wood-burning fireplaces require full chimney structures, masonry support, and strict clearance requirements. Gas fireplace framing involves routing gas lines and planning for either vented or vent-free systems, while electric fireplaces demand careful placement for wiring and cabinetry. The HelloNation article explains that delaying these decisions may lead to exposed utilities, venting misalignment, or limited firebox options, all of which reduce the overall quality of the project.

Placement within the floor plan is equally important. New home fireplace design often calls for the unit to serve as a visual focal point. This requires attention to ceiling heights, centered walls, and proportional balance. Chimney placement in new builds also influences insulation strategies, load-bearing walls, and heating efficiency. Poor planning, as described in the HelloNation feature, can waste energy and limit the intended impact of the fireplace within the home.

Fireplace venting requirements are another crucial factor. Ventless gas fireplaces can disrupt air exchange if not properly configured. In airtight modern homes, combustion air supplies may be needed to prevent backdrafts or the loss of conditioned air. Wood-burning fireplace installation requires chimney placement that prevents smoke backflow and supports thermal efficiency. Coordinating with HVAC design ensures the fireplace functions as intended without compromising indoor air quality.

Building codes for fireplaces guide every step of the process. Clearance distances, chimney heights, combustion air intake, and exhaust standards are regulated to protect both structure and occupants. The HelloNation article stresses that these requirements must be integrated into construction drawings early. When overlooked, builders risk safety issues and costly corrections if inspectors require changes after framing is complete.

Moisture control is another part of fireplace construction planning. Chimney caps, flashing, and roof penetrations must be detailed correctly to prevent water intrusion. Even electric fireplace installation requires forethought in damp areas or exterior walls, where condensation risks can damage finishes or shorten the unit's lifespan. Proper planning helps preserve both the fireplace system and the home's durability.

Finishing details, such as stone, tile, or millwork, are also shaped by early decisions. The weight and thickness of these finishes affect framing and clearances. The HelloNation feature notes that poor coordination may lead to design compromises when structural requirements limit material choices. When planned correctly, builders can transition smoothly from rough construction to polished, customized finishes that enhance the fireplace as a central feature.

The article also points out the financial impact of fireplace planning. Late-stage changes often require vent rerouting, framing adjustments, or structural reinforcement, which drive up costs and delay schedules. By making decisions about type, placement, and venting during the architectural phase, builders maintain both budgets and timelines. This proactive approach ensures homeowners receive a fireplace that is functional, stylish, and completed without disruption.

In conclusion, the HelloNation article underscores that planning for a fireplace in a new construction build is not a minor detail. Fireplace construction planning is central to achieving mechanical reliability, aesthetic appeal, and long-term safety. When architects, contractors, and homeowners collaborate from the start, the result is a fireplace that supports design goals while meeting fireplace venting requirements and building codes for fireplaces.

Planning for a Fireplace in a New Construction Build features insights from Jimmy Smith, Fireplace Expert of North Charleston, South Carolina, in HelloNation.

About HelloNation
HelloNation is a premier media platform that connects readers with trusted professionals and businesses across various industries. Through its innovative "edvertising" approach that blends educational content and storytelling, HelloNation delivers expert-driven articles that inform, inspire, and empower. Covering topics from home improvement and health to business strategy and lifestyle, HelloNation highlights leaders making a meaningful impact in their communities.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/in-hellonation-fireplace-expert-jimmy-smith-of-north-charleston-explains-fireplace-construction-planning-302737314.html

SOURCE HelloNation

data.title: Medpace Holdings, Inc. (MEDP) Shareholders Who Lost Money – Contact Law Offices of Howard G. Smith About Securities Fraud Investigation
data.description:
Medpace Holdings, Inc. (MEDP) Shareholders Who Lost Money – Contact Law Offices of Howard G. Smith About Securities Fraud Investigation

Law Offices of Howard G. Smith announces an investigation on behalf of Medpace Holdings, Inc. (“Medpace” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ: MEDP) investors concerning the Company’s possible violations of federal securities laws.

IF YOU ARE AN INVESTOR WHO SUFFERED A LOSS IN MEDPACE HOLDINGS, INC. (MEDP), CONTACT THE LAW OFFICES OF HOWARD G. SMITH ABOUT POTENTIALLY PURSUING CLAIMS TO RECOVER YOUR LOSS.

Contact the Law Offices of Howard G. Smith to discuss your legal rights by email at howardsmith@howardsmithlaw.com, by telephone at (215) 638-4847 or visit our website at www.howardsmithlaw.com.

What Happened?

On February 9, 2026, Medpace released its fourth quarter 2025 financial results, revealing a book-to-bill ratio of 1.04, well below the Company’s guidance of 1.15. The Company attributed the miss to backlog cancellations being “the highest they’ve been in over a year.”

On this news, Medpace’s stock price fell $84.30, or 15.9%, to close at $446.05 per share on February 10, 2026, thereby injuring investors.

Contact Us To Participate or Learn More:

If you purchased Medpace securities, have information or would like to learn more about these claims, or have any questions concerning this announcement or your rights or interests with respect to these matters, please contact us:

data.title: Oil prices sink, but trade above session lows as cease-fire deal offers two weeks of uncertainty
data.description:
By Myra P. Saefong and Isabel Wang

U.S. oil prices are still over 40% higher than before the Iran war, while Brent crude has risen 31% since late February

President Donald Trump said Washington and Tehran had agreed to a "double sided CEASEFIRE!"

Oil prices were down sharply Wednesday after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week cease-fire, but U.S. and global benchmark crude traded above their sessions lows as Iran maintained control of the Strait of Hormuz.

Iran, meanwhile, has reportedly accused Israel of breaking the cease-fire deal with its attacks on Lebanon. As a result, Iran's Fars news Agency said in a post on X that the passage of oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz has been halted.

Read: Iran moves to put the brakes on reopening the Strait of Hormuz

"It's not surprising to see oil prices come down in the manner that they have," said Tariq Zahir, managing member at Tyche Capital Advisors. However, they are still substantially higher from when the war started, he noted.

This cease fire is 'two weeks of uncertainty, and we could be in the same exact place in two weeks.' Tariq Zahir, Tyche Capital Advisors

"Until we see real clarity on the strait reopening to everyone and the negotiations that start late this week, we do not feel we will see prices continue their downward track to prewar levels," he told MarketWatch.

"We need clarity on the strait, we need clarity on nuclear stockpiles and clarity on ballistic missiles. This cease-fire offers "two weeks of uncertainty, and we could be in the same exact place in two weeks," Zahir added.

U.S. benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude for May delivery (CLK26) (CL.1) was down 15.6%, to trade at $95.30 a barrel, after trading as low as $91.03 Wednesday. It was on track to mark the largest one-day percentage decline since April 27, 2020, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

June Brent crude (BRNM26) (BRN00) was falling 13.1% to $95.02 a barrel, paring its decline for the session after touching an intraday low of $90.40. It was poised for its largest daily percentage fall in a week.

The overall decline in oil prices comes after U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday evening announced in a post on Truth Social that Washington and Tehran had agreed to a cease-fire. The deal would allow for the Strait of Hormuz to reopen for two weeks, while the U.S. would suspend attacks on Iran.

The agreement was reached less than two hours before Trump's deadline of 8 p.m. Eastern time Tuesday, when he warned that Iran's "whole civilization will die" if the Islamic Republic did not accept his demands.

Trump also said that the U.S. and Iran were "very far along with a definitive" truce and referred to Tehran's 10-point proposal, which reportedly includes a guarantee that Iran would not be attacked again by the U.S., as "a workable basis on which to negotiate."

See: Then and now: Comparing where the S&P 500, crude and other markets are today with their prewar situations

Still, key questions remain when it comes to oil, including how quickly transit through the Strait of Hormuz can resume, how long will it take to resupply importers, whether the cease-fire will hold, and whether Iran will "capitalize on the opportunity to assert strength over the strait," said Rob Haworth, senior investment strategy director at U.S. Bank Asset Management.

He pointed out that there's a "physical limitation to normalization" when it comes to Asian supply and demand, because the trip to Asia from the Middle East takes 30 days each way. Also, there's the question of whether infrastructure damage in the Gulf will limit export capabilities if ships arrive to fill and resupply global inventories, said Haworth.

Oil prices remained significantly higher than before the U.S. and Israel first struck Iran at the end of February. WTI crude is still 42% higher than it was at the start of the war, while Brent crude has risen 31% since then, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Bettors on prediction-market platform Polymarket have wagered a 30% chance that traffic through the route will return to normal by the end of April, down from 65% one month ago. (Polymarket has a data partnership with Dow Jones, the publisher of MarketWatch.)

"Markets have been clouded by multiple kinds of uncertainty, from fears of higher inflation and rising interest rates to general unease over geopolitical relations," Dan Coatsworth, head of markets at AJ Bell, wrote in a note on Wednesday.

He said the cease-fire "gives the world a moment to breathe and take stock of events. Unfortunately, there is no guarantee that everything will return to normal."

See: The cease-fire mirage: Here's why stock-market bulls may already be getting ahead of themselves

U.S. benchmark stock indexes were surging on Wednesday. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was up 2.6%, while S&P 500 SPX was rising 2.5% and the Nasdaq Composite COMP added 3.1%.

Read: The real reason stocks are rallying so much after the Iran cease-fire

Nora Redmond contributed.

-Myra P. Saefong -Isabel Wang

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  04-08-26 1331ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Perpetuals Launches Quantum Resilient Security Service to Strengthen Encryption Standards Across Financial Markets
data.description:
• Service designed for financial institutions, trading platforms, payment networks, enterprise security providers, and others who rely on encryption and face inevitable threats from quantum computing

  • Can be integrated into existing systems, enabling organizations to enhance security without affecting integrations, workflows, or regulatory compliance

TOKYO, JP / ACCESS Newswire / April 8, 2026 / Perpetuals.com Ltd (NASDAQ:PDC), the AI-powered financial services company that is leveling the playing field for traders, today announced the launch of Quantum-Resilience-as-a-Service (QRaaS), a new security offering that helps organizations strengthen their encryption in the face of emerging risks posed by quantum computing.

Advances in quantum computing are expected to challenge the security of widely used cryptographic systems. Many of today's encryption methods rely on mathematical problems and random number generation that could become breakable with sufficiently powerful quantum machines.

Researchers at Google Quantum AI have calculated that a superconducting quantum computer could crack the elliptic curve cryptography securing Bitcoin and Ethereum transactions in approximately nine minutes, using fewer than 500,000 physical qubits, hardware already within the scaling range of platforms demonstrated in the lab. At current valuations, 6.9 million Bitcoin are vulnerable to quantum attack right now, and with real-world asset tokenization projected to expand quantum-exposed holdings nearly tenfold by 2030, the threat is growing faster than most institutions are moving to address it.

Perpetuals is meeting that need now, with QRaaS designed for organizations that rely on strong encryption, from financial institutions and payment networks to enterprise security systems and government communications. Perpetuals's QRaaS effectively strengthens the security of every transaction, without altering the end-user experience.

Perpetuals's QRaaS integrates seamlessly with existing systems, including environments built on widely adopted cryptographic standards such as RSA, AES, and TLS. Instead of requiring new algorithms or system-wide modifications, the service strengthens existing key generation processes by supplying quantum-resilient entropy, improving the randomness, unpredictability, and security of cryptographic keys. As part of this offering, Perpetuals has developed multiple integration options around dedicated PCIe-based quantum random number generator (QRNG) hardware and software for direct deployment within client rack infrastructure, generating non-deterministic random material from quantum physical processes. QRaaS is offered through flexible deployment options, including colocation, managed server environments, and an Entropy-as-a-Service API.

"As quantum computing advances, existing encryption standards are increasingly vulnerable. Sensitive data can be harvested today and held for decryption later, creating a real and present security challenge for organizations handling valuable information," said Patrick Gruhn, CEO of Perpetuals. "At the same time, stronger entropy and more resilient key generation are already essential for improving cryptographic security today, regardless of when quantum systems mature. QRaaS enables organizations to strengthen their security immediately, without the need for disruptive changes to existing systems."

QRaaS is hosted in carrier-grade data centers in Germany and the United States, designed to ensure high performance, resilience, and compliance for institutional users. The service includes built-in controls, monitoring, and audit capabilities to support compliance with regulatory and industry standards across all markets.

###

About Perpetuals.com Ltd

Perpetuals.com Ltd (NASDAQ: PDC) is a fintech company developing AI-powered trading products and prediction markets, with a global footprint across the United States, Europe, and Asia. Its mission is to reduce risk through empowering retail users with intuitive, secure, and efficient trading experiences across multiple asset classes.

Perpetuals's proprietary trading platform, Kronos X, combines advanced AI and data analysis. The technology is trained on billions of trades, monitors market activity in real time, identifies patterns for trading and risk decisions, and provides multi-asset coverage with self‑clearing blockchain-based settlement. The company's licensed European Multilateral Trading Facility (MTF) infrastructure and Kronos X multi‑asset exchange platform operate with full MiFID II, MiCA, DORA, and EMIR compliance.

For more information, visit www.perpetuals.com.

Forward-Looking Statements: This press release contains forward-looking statements as defined within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995, including, but not limited to, statements set forth in the company's filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Words such as "expect", "will", "positions", "advancing", "aligning", and other similar expressions may indicate forward-looking statements, though not all forward-looking statements contain such words. These statements reflect the company's current view with respect to future events, are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially, including regulatory approvals, completion of the announced transaction, market conditions, and risks detailed in the company's SEC filings, and are necessarily based upon a number of estimates and assumptions that, while considered reasonable by the company, are inherently subject to significant business, economic, competitive, political, and social uncertainties, and contingencies. Should one or more of these risks or uncertainties materialize, or should the assumptions set out by the company underlying those forward-looking statements prove incorrect, actual results may vary materially from those described herein. Individuals are cautioned that forward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance and accordingly investors are cautioned not to put undue reliance on forward-looking statements due to the inherent uncertainty therein. These forward-looking statements are made as of the date of this press release and the company does not intend, and does not assume any obligation, to update these forward-looking statements, except as required by law.
